---
title: "Video stuck in 640x480."
date: 2008-08-18
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by diablo75 on 2008-08-18
Someone I know has just installed Ubuntu on their system and they are having trouble getting their display to do anything but output 640x480 resolution.  Here is the output of their lspci:

```
01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: S3 Inc. VT8375 [ProSavage8 KM266/KL266]
```

And here is their xorg.conf file:

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver          "kbd"
        Option          "XkbRules"      "xorg"
        Option          "XkbModel"      "pc105"
        Option          "XkbLayout"     "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
        Driver          "mouse"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "Configured Video Device"
        Boardname       "vesa"
        Busid           "PCI:1:0:0"
        Driver          "vesa"
        Screen  0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Configured Monitor"
        Vendorname      "Plug 'n' Play"
        Modelname       "Plug 'n' Play"
  modeline  "1024x768" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
        Gamma   1.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        Device          "Configured Video Device"
        Monitor         "Configured Monitor"
        Defaultdepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth   24
                Virtual 640     480
                Modes           "640x480@60"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
EndSection
Section "Module"
        Load            "glx"
        Load            "GLcore"
        Load            "v4l"
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection
```

What can be done to expand his selection of screen resolutions?

---

### Post by rossjman1 on 2008-08-18
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
run that command and follow the prompts. pressing enter through the menus keeps your previous config.

---
